title: Inhibition Kinetics Measurement
author: Tjusls China
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7y9hpz6
materials: Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader

steps.title: Plate Cleaning and Drying
steps.instruction: Soak the 96-well plates in 75% ethanol and put the container in ultrasonic cleaner for 30min to 1 hour, then use ddH2O to wash these plates several times. Put these clean plates in drying oven at 55°C.

steps.title: Inhibitor and Enzyme Dilutions
steps.instruction: Dilute inhibitors using 100%DMSO to 8mM (a.k.a. 80μM in reaction system) and dilute in 2-time gradients for 12 concentrations. Dilute the enzyme using its buffer to standard concentration (same as the determination of Km).

steps.title: Reaction Setup and Incubation
steps.instruction: Add the reaction system into 96-well plates. Pipet 94 μL protein solution and 1μL inhibitor solution(diluted as 2-time gradients, at least 8 different concentrations), incubated at room temperature for 5 mins, then add substrate CDC-1 5μL.

steps.title: Set Controls
steps.instruction: Set controls. Negative control: 94 μL protein solution，1μL DMSO, 5μL substrate Blank control: 94μL protein buffer，1μL DMSO, 5μL substrate And there are 3 parallel holes for each system.

steps.title: Configure Microplate Reader Program
steps.instruction: Set up the program in Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader. Shake for 10 sec at 654 rpm Kinetic Cycle (to read fluorescent intensity each cycle) Fluorescent measure, 20 cycle, 30sec for each cycle

steps.title: Start Measurement
steps.instruction: Put the plate in Microplate reader, and click Start button.

steps.title: Data Export and Initial Analysis
steps.instruction: When the facility ends testing, save data and import the data of 0-200s into GraphPad Prism Software. Use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” and regulate the number of data to fit R2.

steps.title: Calculate Ir and Fit IC50 Curve
steps.instruction: Move baseline of blank control. Calculate Ir = (1-Vr/V0)*100%. Take average Ir of each inhibitors’ concentrations as Y value, and take log[I] as X value. Then use “nonlinear fit” – “log(inhibitor) vs. normalized response – Variable slope” to fit IC50 curve, and its value would be calculated automatically.

steps.title: Determine Reversible/Irreversible Inhibition Mechanism
steps.instruction: Set up gradient concentrations of protein (revolves around the value in screening system) As well as gradient concentrations of inhibitor (revolves around the value of IC50) Draw the plot of V0-[E] to see the movement of straight line. If they are parallel with each other, the mechanism is irreversible. If they are all through the origin, the mechanism is reversible.

steps.title: Determine Competitive/Noncompetitive Inhibition
steps.instruction: Set up gradient concentrations of substrate (revolves around the value in screening system) As well as gradient concentrations of inhibitor (revolves around the value of IC50). Draw the plot of 1/V0-1/[S] to see the movement of straight line (Lineweaver-Burk plot). If they gather at Y axis, the mechanism is competitive. If they pass the same point in X axis, the mechanism is noncompetitive. If they gather at another point in this dimension, it would be mixed type competition (uncompetitive inhibition).

steps.title: Calculate Ki Value
steps.instruction: After decision of this inhibitor’s mechanism, a constant Ki can be calculated via different equations. [1] For competitive inhibition, For noncompetitive inhibition, For uncompetitive inhibition, Also, we use a website tool (https://bioinfo-abcc.ncifcrf.gov/IC50_Ki_Converter/index.php) to calculate its value automatically.
